description_long: This is a hand tool featuring a robust, rectangular metal head with two flat, opposing blades extending horizontally from a central socket. The head is mounted on a cylindrical wooden handle that appears to be made of light-colored, unfinished wood, showing a natural grain pattern. The metal head is dark, likely steel or iron, with a matte to slightly glossy finish and no visible decorative elements. The handle is straight, smooth, and tapers slightly towards the end, providing a comfortable grip for manual use. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for heavy-duty tasks such as breaking or splitting materials in urban maintenance or construction settings. No visible branding, engravings, or labels are present on either the handle or the head.
description: A utilitarian axe with a rectangular, double-edged dark metal head and a straight, light wooden handle. The design is simple and functional, with no visible markings or decorations.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight wooden handle and the rectangular metal head with both blades visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the circular cross-section of the handle and the symmetrical, flat blades extending horizontally from the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, displaying the handle and the rectangular head with both blades visible.
description_view_3: View from the handle's end, emphasizing the round handle and the flat, wide spread of the metal blades.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, light-colored, smooth surface, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Rectangular, double-edged metal head with flat, wide blades extending horizontally; no beard or poll, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional socket mounting with the handle inserted through the central hole of the metal head.
